questions.text: Who is credited with the first observation of cells?
questions.explanation: Robert Hooke first described cells while observing cork tissue.

questions.text: Which organelle is called the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria generate ATP through cellular respiration.

questions.text: Which structure controls the movement of substances into and out of the cell?
questions.explanation: The cell membrane regulates transport and maintains the cell environment.

questions.text: Which organelle is responsible for protein synthesis?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes assemble amino acids into proteins.

questions.text: Which of these is a feature of prokaryotic cells?
questions.explanation: Prokaryotes do not have membrane-bound organelles such as a nucleus.

questions.text: Which organelle contains chlorophyll and carries out photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts capture light energy and produce sugars in plant cells.

questions.text: Anton von Leeuwenhoek first saw and described a:
questions.explanation: Leeuwenhoek saw living cells, whereas Robert Hooke discovered dead cells in cork.

questions.text: The Cell Theory was formulated by:
questions.explanation: Proposed by Schleiden (botanist) and Schwann (zoologist).

questions.text: Who explained that cells arise from pre-existing cells (Omnis cellula-e cellula)?
questions.explanation: Rudolf Virchow gave final shape to the cell theory.

questions.text: The smallest cell is ________ and the largest isolated single cell is ________.
questions.explanation: Mycoplasmas are only 0.3 μm in length.

questions.text: Bacteria have small circular DNA outside the genomic DNA called:
questions.explanation: Plasmid DNA confers unique phenotypic characters like antibiotic resistance.

questions.text: A specialized differentiated form of cell membrane found in prokaryotes is:
questions.explanation: Mesosomes are extensions of plasma membrane into the cell.

questions.text: Prokaryotic ribosomes are of ________ type.
questions.explanation: They are composed of 50S and 30S subunits.

questions.text: Several ribosomes attached to a single mRNA form a chain called:
questions.explanation: Ribosomes of a polysome translate mRNA into proteins.

questions.text: The improved model of cell membrane structure proposed by Singer and Nicolson is:
questions.explanation: Describes the quasi-fluid nature enabling lateral movement of proteins.

questions.text: The primary function of the plasma membrane is:
questions.explanation: It is selectively permeable to molecules on either side of it.

questions.text: The movement of water by diffusion across a membrane is called:
questions.explanation: Water moves from higher concentration to lower concentration.

questions.text: Algae have a cell wall made of:
options: Cellulose, galactans, mannans and calcium carbonate
questions.explanation: Algal cell wall composition is distinct from higher plants.

questions.text: The layer which holds neighbouring cells together is:
questions.explanation: It is mainly composed of calcium pectate.

questions.text: The endomembrane system includes:
options: ER, Golgi complex, Lysosomes and Vacuoles
questions.explanation: These organelles have coordinated functions.

questions.text: Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum (RER) is actively involved in:
questions.explanation: RER has surface ribosomes for protein production.

questions.text: Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum (SER) is the major site for synthesis of:
questions.explanation: SER synthesizes lipids and steroidal hormones in animal cells.

questions.text: Densely stained structures near the nucleus observed by Camillo Golgi are:
questions.explanation: Consists of flat, disc-shaped cisternae.

questions.text: The important site of formation of glycoproteins and glycolipids is:
questions.explanation: Golgi apparatus packages and processes materials.

questions.text: Lysosomes are rich in which type of enzymes?
questions.explanation: These enzymes are active at acidic pH.

questions.text: In plant cells, the vacuole is bound by a single membrane called:
questions.explanation: It can occupy up to 90% of the plant cell volume.

questions.text: The inner compartment of mitochondria is called:
questions.explanation: Infoldings of the inner membrane are called cristae.

questions.text: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and which other pigments?
questions.explanation: Pigments responsible for trapping light energy.

questions.text: The space limited by the inner membrane of the chloroplast is called the:
questions.explanation: Contains enzymes for synthesis of carbohydrates and proteins.

questions.text: The ribosomes of eukaryotic cells are:
questions.explanation: Composed of 60S and 40S subunits.

questions.text: Centrosome contains two cylindrical structures called:
questions.explanation: They are surrounded by pericentriolar materials.

questions.text: The core of cilia/flagella with a 9+2 arrangement of microtubules is the:
questions.explanation: Microtubules are made of tubulin protein.

questions.text: The nucleus was first described by:
questions.explanation: Described in 1831.

questions.text: The nucleoplasm contains which of the following?
questions.explanation: Chromatin contains DNA and histone proteins.

questions.text: A chromosome with the centromere slightly away from the middle is:
questions.explanation: Results in one shorter and one longer arm.

questions.text: Acrocentric chromosomes have centromere:
questions.explanation: Results in one extremely short arm.

questions.text: Minute membrane-bound vesicles containing various enzymes in cells are:
questions.explanation: Example: Peroxisomes.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT part of the cytoskeleton?
questions.explanation: Cisternae belong to the ER or Golgi apparatus.

questions.text: The name 'chromatin' for nuclear material was given by:
questions.explanation: Flemming used basic dyes to stain nuclear material.

questions.text: The primary constriction of a chromosome is called the:
questions.explanation: Sides contain disc-shaped structures called kinetochores.
